---
title: Results
---

All results are on **simulated truth**: `msprime` admixture scenarios with a census event
placed just older than the admixture pulse, so every lineage is unambiguously in a source
population and the true local ancestry is known per haplotype
([`simulate_admixture`](../api/simulate_admixture.html),
[`local_ancestry_truth`](../api/local_ancestry_truth.html)). Figures are embedded from the
[notebooks](../notebooks/painting.ipynb) — the code that produced them is one click away.

## Accuracy & calibration

tspaint's distinguishing value is a **calibrated** soft posterior, not raw accuracy. Predicted
`P(A)` tracks the empirical frequency (points near the diagonal):

{{< embed ../notebooks/calibration.ipynb#fig-reliability >}}

Discrimination is strong at recent-to-moderate admixture and decays at old admixture — but not
from tree-inference error: under present-day sampling, admixed lineages coalesce **among
themselves** before an old pulse, severing the query→reference genealogical link. This is a
coalescent limit; the crossover age scales with $N_e$ and the sampling scheme.

{{< embed ../notebooks/calibration.ipynb#fig-accuracy-age >}}

## Fragmentation & tract-length fidelity

Downstream admixture-pulse dating reads the **segment-length distribution**, so spurious short
opposite-ancestry calls bias the inferred pulse *older*. Naive `argmax` of the posterior
over-fragments; because the posterior is calibrated, a modest confidence **deadband**
(`paint(deadband=…)` / [`hard_segments`](../api/output.hard_segments.html)) suppresses the
low-confidence flips and recovers the true switch density:

{{< embed ../notebooks/fragmentation.ipynb#fig-switchdensity >}}

so the inferred tract-length distribution matches the truth:

{{< embed ../notebooks/fragmentation.ipynb#fig-tractlen >}}

Over 6 seeds (true ARG, strong structure, `T_admix`=200, 5 Mb), the switch-density ratio
(inferred / true) is **1.95 ± 0.58** for naive argmax but **0.99 ± 0.01** with a deadband of
0.4 — recovering the true distribution near-perfectly, and beating RFMix's native segmentation
(1.35 ± 0.22).

## Head-to-head vs. RFMix

Against the field-standard segment incumbent
([RFMix v2](https://www.cell.com/ajhg/fulltext/S0002-9297(13)00276-8); wired as
[`rfmix_paint`](../api/compare.rfmix_paint.html)), tspaint **matches balanced accuracy** across
regimes while staying calibrated where RFMix saturates to 0/1:

| regime | rfmix | tspaint | nearest-ref baseline |
|---|---|---|---|
| strong structure (Ne 1e3, split 5e3) | 0.99 / conf 1.00 | 0.99 / conf **0.68** | 0.99 / conf 1.00 |
| weak structure (Ne 1e4, split 2e3) | 0.78–0.85 / conf 0.92–1.0 | 0.77–0.80 / conf **0.10** | 0.79–0.82 / conf 1.0 |

*(balanced accuracy / mean confidence)*. Neither dominates on accuracy; tspaint's edge is the
calibrated soft posterior + readable `Q` / credibility.

## Inferred ARGs & the horizontal BP smoother

On a **true** ARG the per-tree posteriors are clean and the deadband is near-optimal. On an
**inferred** (tsinfer) ARG, tree inference scatters spurious breakpoints a per-position
threshold cannot filter — the horizontal BP smoother
([`paint(smooth=True)`](../api/bp.bp_paint.html)) suppresses them. Without smoothing:

{{< embed ../notebooks/bp_smoother.ipynb#fig-bp-plain >}}

with smoothing:

{{< embed ../notebooks/bp_smoother.ipynb#fig-bp-smooth >}}

Quantified over seeds, the BP smoother is **redundant on the true ARG** but **wins decisively
on inferred ARGs** (breakpoint F1 ≈ 0.71 → 0.98 at `T_admix`=500):

{{< embed ../notebooks/bp_smoother.ipynb#fig-bp >}}

## Admixture dating: rate through time

A separate deliverable (see [admixture dating](dating.qmd)): the time-inhomogeneous estimator
fits the directional cross-ancestry rates `q_AB(t)`, `q_BA(t)`. On a clean split the rate is ~0
more recently than the divergence and rises across it — here the inferred onset lands within
~1 % of the true split time:

{{< embed ../notebooks/dating.ipynb#fig-dating-profile >}}

Across the validation rungs (true ARG) it sharpens the onset toward the true split, resolves a
pulse / asymmetric pulse / ongoing migration, and survives on inferred (SINGER) ARGs — where the
binding axis is calibration rather than point accuracy. Feeding `Q(t)` back into the painting is
*not* on the default path: it adds no painting accuracy, only sharper (possibly mis-calibrated)
confidence at ~4–5× cost, so painting and dating stay side by side.

## Reproducing

Every figure above is computed in a [notebook](../notebooks/painting.ipynb); the end-to-end drivers live in
`tspaint.experiments` (e.g. [`age_sweep`](../api/experiments.age_sweep.html),
[`fragmentation_experiment`](../api/experiments.fragmentation_experiment.html),
[`bp_vs_deadband_experiment`](../api/bp.bp_vs_deadband_experiment.html)), with the dating
estimator in [`tspaint.dating`](dating.qmd).
